title: Entity Management - Relation Events
description: JSON Schema definitions for Entity Management domain events related to many-to-many relation lifecycle operations. These events are published via SNS when many-to-many (NN) relation records are created or deleted. Derived from IErpPostCreateManyToManyRelationHook and IErpPostDeleteManyToManyRelationHook interfaces, orchestrated by RecordHookManager. Only ManyToMany relations emit these events; OneToOne and OneToMany mutations are handled via record update events.

eventEnvelope.description: Common event envelope shared by all Entity Management relation domain events. Provides tracing, versioning, and source identification for SNS-published messages.

eventType.description: Fully qualified event type following the naming convention {domain}.{entity}.{action}.

correlationId.description: UUID for distributed tracing and request correlation. Enables idempotent event processing across consumers.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp indicating when the event was produced.

source.description: Constant identifying the Entity Management service as the event source.

data.description: Domain-specific event payload. Structure varies by eventType.

relationCreatedEvent.description: Published when a many-to-many relation record is created in the Entity Management service. Derived from IErpPostCreateManyToManyRelationHook.OnPostCreate(string relationName, Guid originId, Guid targetId) which is invoked by RecordHookManager.ExecutePostCreateManyToManyRelationHook after a successful NN relation insert. Both originId and targetId are non-nullable as they are required Guid parameters in the source interface.

data.description: Payload for many-to-many relation creation events.

relationName.description: Name of the many-to-many relation. Maximum 63 characters, enforced by EntityRelationManager validation (PostgreSQL column name width limit). Corresponds to the 'relationName' parameter in IErpPostCreateManyToManyRelationHook.OnPostCreate.

originId.description: GUID of the origin record in the many-to-many relation. Non-nullable, derived from the Guid originId parameter in IErpPostCreateManyToManyRelationHook.OnPostCreate.

targetId.description: GUID of the target record in the many-to-many relation. Non-nullable, derived from the Guid targetId parameter in IErpPostCreateManyToManyRelationHook.OnPostCreate.

createdBy.description: GUID of the user who created the relation record. Resolved from the JWT claims in the Lambda event context.

relationDeletedEvent.description: Published when a many-to-many relation record is deleted from the Entity Management service. Derived from IErpPostDeleteManyToManyRelationHook.OnPostDelete(string relationName, Guid? originId, Guid? targetId) which is invoked by RecordHookManager.ExecutePostDeleteManyToManyRelationHook after a successful NN relation removal. CRITICAL: originId and targetId are nullable (Guid?) in the source interface, meaning bulk deletes or cascade operations may not identify specific records.

data.description: Payload for many-to-many relation deletion events.

relationName.description: Name of the many-to-many relation. Maximum 63 characters. Corresponds to the 'relationName' parameter in IErpPostDeleteManyToManyRelationHook.OnPostDelete.

originId.description: GUID of the origin record in the many-to-many relation, or null. Nullable because the source interface uses Guid? originId in IErpPostDeleteManyToManyRelationHook.OnPostDelete, allowing bulk or cascade deletes where the specific origin is not identified.

targetId.description: GUID of the target record in the many-to-many relation, or null. Nullable because the source interface uses Guid? targetId in IErpPostDeleteManyToManyRelationHook.OnPostDelete, allowing bulk or cascade deletes where the specific target is not identified.

deletedBy.description: GUID of the user who deleted the relation record. Resolved from the JWT claims in the Lambda event context.
